text: "I think there’s a lot of reasons for Brazil’s issues, and I I agree, they’ve recently begun to really emphasize building a team around one player - Neymar.IMO it’s just lazy coaching by the coaches or probably a mix of not putting the right players who can play together as a unit.I also think they’ve gotten away from playing a fun game.Brazil in 02 was a masterpiece because everyone of those players showed up to work hard and play hard and entertain.I think their joy and trust in each other just made a solid team.I also think you had so many superstars with some really solid leaders who balanced each other out.Ronaldo in his later playing days was criticized for being a bit lazy and he kinda was but he could put that ball in the next like crazy and his teammates hauled ass for him.I think they overrelied on Neymar who I will say I never questioned his effort for his country. I don’t remember seeing Brazil play like Belgium did in 18 or Germany in 14, for example.I didn’t say that Brazil is currently bad, and I mentioned that Ancelotti just started training the team. text: "The weaknesses in Conmebol qualifying are showing up. Brazil has been a shell of itself for years relying entirely on individual brilliance, Chile/Peru are trotting out retirement teams, Colombia is on its way there, and Uruguay is very hot and cold.These deficiencies lead to disjointed offenses that bunkering team like Ecuador and Paraguay can smother on their way to qualification.Now that they’re playing teams who have had a month to develop chemistry they’re getting exposed. The USA and Ivory Coast bulldozed going forward against the defensive darlings.On the European side it feels more like they aren’t trying as hard as their competition coupled with struggling to deal with the increase in size/athleticism. They also can’t deal with the heat despite it being very mild so far, in 2 weeks these teams will be dying despite having a full week between matches."

text: "I think so far UEFA and CONMEBOL teams have either underestimated their opponents or are relying on tactics that are easily exposed by teams that play differently.For Czechia and Bosnia, both teams played similarly, relying on their physical strength and defensive setups. While Canada took some time to finally equalize, they also weren't very clinical in front of goal, and could've easily scored another two or three goals. Bosnia largely sat back and did not look to take more chances. Czechia relied heavily on their height and set pieces (both of Bosnia and Czechia's goals were scored off set pieces), but really didn't give Korea a huge challenge with creating non-set piece chances. Korea is also technically a much better team. Tbf, both of these UEFA teams barely made the World Cup, so they aren't necessarily the best of the best.Paraguay was simply outplayed in almost every aspect. I think people like to criticize the US only because they're the US, but the Americans played very well and were clearly the better team. People just need to realize that the US is probably better than they think.For the cases of the Swiss, the Turks, the Spanish, and the Belgians, I felt like all these teams underestimated their opponents. The AFC and AFCON are stronger than people think. While I think Switzerland, Turkiye, and Belgium are maybe not the best UEFA teams, they still have very strong teams and can make a good run into the knockout stages, and should be beating Qatar, Australia, and Egypt. Qatar kept pushing and scored late, and I think that was due to the Swiss falling asleep and keeping their guard down because "Oh they're only Qatar, what can they do." Tbf to the Turks, Australia did park the bus once they took the lead, but they also took advantage of their chances. It's also Turkiye's first World Cup appearance in over two decades, so it's possible there were some nerves, but with the players they have, one would think they should be beating Australia. Egypt also has great talent, and I think Belgium overlooked this. It wasn't until they made subs that they forced an OG, but Belgium's golden generation is definitely fading. On the other hand, not trying to discredit Cape Verde, but there is no excuse for Spain. They absolutely should be beating Cape Verde, and their performance was shocking. Spain didn't play with a ton of urgency until they brought on Lamine and Williams, which are two players they really shouldn't even be playing to begin with against Cape Verde. Hats off to Cape Verde, though.Ecuador, the Netherlands, Brazil, and Uruguay played teams that are simply stronger than people think. Japan was relentless, and is the best team in the AFC, and while I believe the Dutch should've won, Japan is a team more than capable of reaching the Round of 16. Same case with Morocco and Brazil. Morocco is a very good team and people have taken notice. Brazil and Uruguay are also not what they once were. Brazil is much more high profile, so people know about how they haven't been performing, but Uruguay were grouped in 2022, and no longer have Suarez and Cavani to rely on. These two are simply not as good as they once were. Ecuador looked evenly matched against the Ivory Coast, and I honestly think it was just a battle of who wanted it more. Both teams were unlucky with hitting the woodwork multiple times, but the Ivory Coast thrashed Korea 4-0, beat Scotland and France by a goal in their warm-up games; this is a team that is underrated.It's a mix of underestimation by UEFA and CONMEBOL, some teams (Brazil and Uruguay for example) not being as good as they once were, and other teams just being stronger tactically than in the past and people are only starting to take notice (like Korea and USA)"
label: analysis

text: "I guess I'm taking issue with your focus on scoring rate. That is not the sole metric with which to judge strikers - particularly with the way the game is evolving over the last 10 or so years.I absolutely agree that France didnt win the world cup because or Giroud. Equally, I think they'd have struggled to win without him leading the line in the way that he did. Those two things are not contradictory.He played every game and that attack may well not have clicked in the way it did, without his ability to link it all together - something that is missed when solely considering scoring rates or even g/a data.To say, at his peak, he was no better than Watkins is wild imo. And that's not because I don't think Watkins is quality, but he's not delivered at the same level."
label: analysis

text: "Watkins has delivered at the same level Giroud.Watkins' goal against the Netherlands is a single greater contribution than any moment Giroud ever came up with for France.Watkins is just unlucky he plays at the same time as Harry Kane. The question we need to ask for a fair comparison is what Watkins could have done leading the line in an elite national team that suited his qualities and played to his strengths, like Giroud had.I think if you put Ollie Watkins in Spain's Euro 2024 team they win that tournament even easier and Watkins grabs quite a number of goals, for example.When Giroud was at his best for Arsenal they were an underperforming team that were finishing in similar league positions to what Villa are now at a time when the league was significantly weaker (lost the league to Leicester).Watkins is one of the main men for Villa in the club's best period since the 80s where they've dramatically overachieved at a time the league is at its strongest.I agree Giroud was important to France but strikers who don't score much but can play with their back to goal and link play are not worth the amount of reverence you're giving Giroud. That profile of player is everywhere. Dominic Solanke and Calvert-Lewin are of that ilk.If a player like that is so important then he would've had a better club career and an elite team would've made an effort to sign him.I go back to the fact that he was very lucky in his timing. His qualities perfectly suited the French national team and allowed him to piggy back off better players and have a career he otherwise wouldn't have had. You can see that by the disparity between his club career and international career.The reality is that Giroud would hardly have played for France had Benzema not had all the issues off-pitch issues and fallings out. "
